text.title: Trello Kanban Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Trello Kanban. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Five columns named: Backlog / Ready / In Progress / Review / Done. Extras = scope creep.
rule.rationale: More columns blur stage meaning; five is the kanban literature standard for solo-to-small teams.

rule.statement: WIP limits applied: Ready ≤8, In Progress ≤ team_size × 2, Review ≤5. Violated WIP = priority chaos.
rule.rationale: WIP limits enforce focus; without them kanban is a wish list.

rule.statement: ≤8 labels; labels are categories (bug / feat / ops / sales / etc.), not statuses.
rule.rationale: Label proliferation kills filter consistency + visual scanning.

rule.statement: ≤2 Power-Ups installed. Each Power-Up is a new tax: data lock-in + maintenance.
rule.rationale: Free Trello = 1 Power-Up; paid = unlimited but each is a coupling. Cap discipline ≤2 to keep the board portable.

rule.statement: Butler rules documented in the board description. Undocumented rules = magic next quarter.
rule.rationale: Butler-rule sprawl is invisible technical debt; documentation surfaces it.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-trello-kanban.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
